_instructions.usage: Fill in every field below with real project data, then seed it with: npx tsx scripts/seed-from-json.ts path/to/this-file.json
rules: Only use pre-existing database model fields verified against schema.prisma.
Ensure strict compliance with all model relations, enums, and required values.
Leave all image and document storage_url and url as empty string '' — add via admin Media panel.

project.name: Ajnara The Belvedere
project.address: Plot No. SC-01/A1-Alpha, Sector 79, Noida, Uttar Pradesh 201305
project.tagline: Stalled Neoclassical Grandeur under NCLT CIRP
project.description: A structurally complete but legally mired neoclassical high-rise project by Ajnara India Ltd, currently undergoing active NCLT bankruptcy.
project.long_description: Ajnara The Belvedere is a neoclassical high-rise project designed by renowned architect Hafeez Contractor on a 4.78-acre site in Sector 79's Sports City. Rising G+26 floors across 7 towers, the project features double-height lobbies and premium layouts. However, the project is severely mired in an active Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) since September 2022 due to a default of ₹50 Crore in homebuyer advances and over ₹165 Crore in outstanding land dues to the Noida Authority. Consequently, registries are completely blocked and standard home financing is unavailable, rendering it a highly speculative distressed asset.
marketing_claims: Located in central Sector 79 Sports City corridor
project.project_risk_flag: Extreme: Developer in NCLT bankruptcy since Sep 2022, CBI investigation, frozen registries, and ₹165 Cr outstanding Noida Authority dues.
registry_embargo_reasons: Active Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) under NCLT.
Unpaid Sports City land lease dues exceeding ₹165 Crore.
CBI fraud investigations into the developer's bank-allotment nexus.

builder.name: Ajnara India Ltd (In Bankruptcy)
builder.tagline: Struggling NCR builder under active insolvency
builder.company_overview: Ajnara India Ltd is a legacy mid-market developer established in 1991. The group built numerous townships across Noida, Greater Noida, and Ghaziabad. However, severe fund diversion and systematic defaults led to the group being dragged into active NCLT bankruptcy (CIRP) in September 2022, following complaints by 113 homebuyers of its Ambrosia project.

unit_types.name: 3 BHK (Compact)
unit_types.subtitle: Compact 3 BHK with neoclassical windows
unit_types.description: Standard 3 BHK layout featuring moderate living space, three balconies, and standard vitrified floor finishes.

unit_types.name: 4 BHK (Imperial)
unit_types.subtitle: Spacious 4 BHK with servant quarter
unit_types.description: Premium 4 BHK featuring double-height master balconies, distinct entrance lobby, and dedicated servant suite with service access.
whats_included: Laminated wood floors in master bedroom

connectivity.name: Noida Sector 101 Metro Station (Aqua Line)
connectivity.notes: Aqua line metro connectivity to Central Noida

connectivity.name: Dadri Road
connectivity.notes: Key link road connecting Sector 79 to primary arterial roads

assumptions: Pricing based on heavily discounted secondary cash transactions.
Registries are completely blocked; transaction is based on builder ledger transfers under IRP control.
Stamp duty subject to government registry circular updates.

payment_plan.notes: Traditional bank mortgages are entirely blocked. All funds are routed through specialized escrow control.

decision_profile.decision_thesis: Ajnara The Belvedere is a highly distressed neoclassical asset under active NCLT bankruptcy, offering deep structural discounts (approx ₹10,000/sqft) but carrying severe title, registry, and litigation risks.
why_buy: Extremely cheap entry price: ₹10,000/sqft is nearly half the price of compliant Sector 79 assets.
Neoclassical architecture designed by Hafeez Contractor looks physically premium.
Low spatial density: 128 units/acre across 4.78 acres.
why_avoid: Active NCLT Corporate Insolvency (CIRP) since September 2022 with assets under court receiver control.
Absolute registry block due to massive developer defaults of ₹165 Cr in land lease dues.
CBI fraud investigations into the developer's bank-allotment nexus.
decision_profile.best_for: Speculative, risk-tolerant cash buyers looking for extreme physical structure value.
decision_profile.not_ideal_for: End-users, first-time homebuyers, or anyone requiring bank financing (which is 100% blocked).

persona_profile.family_stage: Wealth-seeking empty nesters or commercial syndicates
persona_profile.motivation_note: Aims to acquire completed structural assets at massive discounts, willing to wait out the multi-year bankruptcy resolution.

recommendation_profile.primary_thesis: Ajnara The Belvedere is a highly toxic real estate asset. Despite its premium Hafeez Contractor designs and structural completion, the developer's bankruptcy and colossal land dues create an unpredictable legal quagmire.
recommendation_profile.end_use_thesis: End-users should avoid this project completely. Handovers are legally stalled and registrations cannot be executed for the foreseeable future.
recommendation_profile.investment_thesis: Only suitable as a speculative cash-pool play for seasoned investors who can afford to lock up liquidity for 5-7 years while the tribunal coordinates a successor builder.
recommendation_profile.family_thesis: Completely unsuitable for families due to legal paralyzation and unstable association governance.
recommendation_profile.investor_thesis: High risk of long-term capital lockup with zero rental operations.
recommendation_profile.luxury_thesis: Beautiful neoclassical aesthetics are completely overshadowed by structural and maintenance stagnant states.
recommendation_profile.risk_thesis: Extreme operational, legal, and liquidity risks.
walk_away_conditions: Unwillingness to deal with direct NCLT litigation committees.
Any requirement of clean title registrations before purchase.
negotiation_leverage: Leverage the developer's active bankruptcy to negotiate massive cash discounts (up to 40% below builder ledger values).
Benchmark against Gaur Sportswood to highlight the complete legal safety delta.
recommendation_profile.timeline_advice: Do not buy. Wait for NCLT to finalize a complete corporate takeover by a strong successor builder.

competitors.competitor_advantage: 100% paid land dues, flawless registries, and full OCs.
competitors.verdict: Gaur Sportswood wins on every safety, legal, and operational metric; Ajnara is only for high-risk speculative cash pools.
